spec_match.rationale: Reads navigator.cpuPerformance as a numeric attribute and uses hardwareConcurrency/deviceMemory only for fallback estimation.

interactive.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP adjusted both sliders, ran calibration, observed chart rows, recommendation output, and a thermal drift value.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page still matches the index promise of a real worker workload and tier-aware pool recommendation.

edge_cases.rationale: The calibrator now reruns the measured best pool under sustained load and folds the drift result into a measured+tier thermal-aware recommendation.

references.rationale: The page now embeds the ChromeStatus feature link and WICG CPU Performance draft.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout and no raw hex literals were found.

runtime_evidence.rationale: Fresh Chrome DevTools MCP v152 sweep loaded the copied concept, exercised every detected control, found named controls, and reported no post-triage runtime/action failure.

conformance.rationale: A v152 suite now carries the same feature-ID/spec contract without modifying the immutable source-milestone suite.

summary: Worker Pool Calibrator now uses navigator.cpuPerformance, shows ChromeStatus/WICG references, and reports sustained-load drift alongside the measured best pool. Chrome DevTools MCP rechecked the page with no console errors.
